text:
Aranya Digital Services Ltd — Prior Cost Programmes (last 3 years; to avoid double-counting)

FY23 — "Project Lean Ops"
- Real-estate first wave: exited 2 leased floors (Pune, Noida); ~₹22 Cr run-rate saved. - Travel policy tightening post-pandemic; ~₹14 Cr saved. STATUS: completed, savings in current baseline. FY24 — "Project Margin Guard" (see T3_20 calibration for realised vs plan)
- Vendor/subcontractor consolidation (14 → 6 panel): planned ₹48 Cr, realised ₹39.5 Cr run-rate. - Cloud FinOps wave 1: planned ₹22 Cr, realised ₹24.6 Cr. - Software licence reclaim: planned ₹15 Cr, realised ₹9.2 Cr (M365 E5 downgrade deferred). - Real-estate seat consolidation: planned ₹18 Cr, realised ₹6 Cr (SEZ lock-in + exit penalties). - STATUS: largely completed; realised savings already in FY25 baseline. Do NOT re-claim these.

₹18 Cr, realised ₹6 Cr (SEZ lock-in + exit penalties). - STATUS: largely completed; realised savings already in FY25 baseline. Do NOT re-claim these. In-flight (FY25, partial year — exclude the booked portion from new opportunity)
- Network single-carrier consolidation (Airtel + Tata Comms): ~₹8 Cr targeted, ~₹3 Cr booked YTD. - Bench reduction / utilisation push: people-cost lever, tracked separately from third-party cost-out. Implication
- New FY26 opportunities should be sized on the residual addressable pool after the above. - Cloud and licence levers still have headroom; subcontractor rate harmonisation can be deepened;
  real-estate remains constrained by SEZ status.
